---
title: "Ramin Akbari"
toc: false
page-layout: full
---

::: {.grid}

::: {.g-col-12 .g-col-md-8}
I am a Ph.D. student in Computer Science and Engineering at Michigan State University, advised by [Dr. Vishnu Boddeti](https://vishnu.boddeti.net/){.advisor-link}. I work on statistical machine learning with a primary focus on the mathematics of controlling representations. My research aims to develop algorithms that mitigate bias in AI models, improve fairness, protect data privacy, and advance safe and trustworthy AI systems.

I also have background in computational mechanics, specifically working with the eXtended Finite Element Method (XFEM), computational homogenization and the constitutive modeling of hyper-elastic material.

* **Ph.D. Student in Computer Science** | Michigan State University
* **M.Sc. in Structural Mechanics** | Sharif University of Technology
* **B.Sc. in Civil Engineering** | University of Tehran

<br>

[<i class="bi bi-github"></i> GitHub](https://github.com/ramin-akbari){.btn .btn-outline-primary}
[<i class="bi bi-mortarboard"></i> Google Scholar](https://scholar.google.com/citations?user=Q2kM_tcAAAAJ&hl=en){.btn .btn-outline-primary}
[<i class="bi bi-file-earmark-person"></i> CV](cv.pdf){.btn .btn-outline-primary}
[<i class="bi bi-linkedin"></i> LinkedIn](https://www.linkedin.com/in/ramin-akbari-a7580025b/){.btn .btn-outline-primary}
[<i class="bi bi-envelope"></i> Email](mailto:akbarigh@msu.edu){.btn .btn-outline-dark }

:::

::: {.g-col-12 .g-col-md-4}
![](ramin2.jpg){width="85%" .rounded}
:::

:::
<br>


## 📢 News 

::: {.grid style="row-gap: 0.2rem;"}

::: {.g-col-2}
**Jan 2026**
:::
::: {.g-col-10}
Extended my internship at **Sartorius** for the spring semester.
:::
::: {.g-col-2}
**Sep 2025**
:::
::: {.g-col-10}
Our paper *Obliviator Reveals the Cost of Nonlinear Guardedness in Concept Erasure* was accepted to **NeurIPS 2025**!
:::
::: {.g-col-2}
**Aug 2025**
:::
::: {.g-col-10}
Started my internship at **Sartorius** in Ann Arbor.
:::
::: {.g-col-2}
**Jun 2025**
:::
::: {.g-col-10}
Our paper *Homomorphically Encrypted Biometric Template Fusion and Matching* was accepted to **TBIOM**!
:::

:::

## 📝 Selected Publications
::: {.grid}

::: {.g-col-12 .g-col-md-4}
![](/posts/obliviator/teaser.png){width="100%" .rounded}
:::

::: {.g-col-12 .g-col-md-8}
<span style="font-size: 1.15em;">**Obliviator Reveals the Cost of Nonlinear Guardedness in Concept Erasure**</span><br>
<u>Ramin Akbari</u>\*, Milad Afshari\*, Vishnu Boddeti<br>
*Advances in Neural Information Processing Systems (NeurIPS), Dec 2025*

Concept erasure aims to remove unwanted attributes from learned representations. Current methods fail to guard against nonlinear adversaries because they do not capture the statistical dependencies between unwanted concepts and learned representations. We introduce Obliviator, a method that captures nonlinear statistical dependencies to successfully guard against nonlinear adversaries. By using an iterative approach, Obliviator prevents mode-collapse and preserves as much information as possible during erasure, achieving a strong benchmark on the utility-erasure trade-off.


[<i class="bi bi-file-richtext"></i> Blog](posts/obliviator/index.html){.btn .btn-outline-primary}
[<i class="bi bi-file-earmark-pdf"></i> arXiv](https://arxiv.org/abs/2603.07529){.btn .btn-outline-primary }
[<i class="bi bi-github"></i> Code](https://github.com/ramin-akbari/Obliviator){.btn .btn-outline-dark }
[<i class="bi bi-journal-check"></i> OpenReview](https://openreview.net/forum?id=GcjpjIHDZn){.btn .btn-outline-success }
[<i class="bi bi-easel"></i> Poster](/posts/obliviator/poster.png){.btn .btn-outline-success }
[<i class="bi bi-file-slides"></i> Slides](posts/obliviator/nips-slides/index.html){.btn .btn-outline-success }
:::

---
